events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need diagnose fault: splice outside box, in-service, commercial electrical. NEC 110.14 covers electrical connections - splices must be in approved boxes, enclosures etc. Splice outside box is non-compliant, likely fire/shock hazard, fail decision, risk high/critical? Splice outside box exposed can cause short, arcing, shock. In-service operating asset. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant, maybe degraded? Since in-service could be degraded but observed condition is splice outside box (installation defect). Use installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete 100 (in-service). value 0. confidence high 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty, sound_source empty string. findings: unenclosed splice, violation of NEC 110.14, shock/fire hazard. actions: de-energize circuit, relocate splice to listed junction box with proper enclosure, inspect all terminations/splices, retest before re-energizing. rationale. references: NEC 110.14. Need ensure JSON valid.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",

  "findings": [
    "Splice located outside of an approved enclosure/junction box",
    "Violation of NEC 110.14 requiring splices and terminations to be made in listed enclosures",
    "Exposure to mechanical damage, moisture, and accidental contact creating shock and fire hazard"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize the affected circuit immediately",
    "Relocate the splice into a listed junction box or enclosure with proper cover",
    "Verify conductor insulation condition and remake splice with approved connector/device",
    "Inspect adjacent terminations and splices for similar defects",
    "Retest and obtain inspection approval before returning to service"
  ],
  "rationale": "An in-service splice found outside a box is an installation defect and code violation. NEC 110.14 requires electrical connections to be made in approved enclosures to ensure safe, reliable terminations. The exposed splice presents shock, arcing, and fire risk, so the installation fails acceptance.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14"
  ]
}
